events.title: US Postal Service Act Signed
events.summary: President George Washington signed the Postal Service Act, establishing a national post office.
events.details: This foundational act created the United States Post Office Department. It ensured cheap, reliable communication and fostered national unity by facilitating the spread of information across the country.

events.title: Rossini's "The Barber of Seville" Premieres
events.summary: Gioachino Rossini's masterpiece comic opera, "The Barber of Seville," premiered in Rome.
events.details: Despite a difficult opening night, the opera quickly became a resounding success. It remains one of the most frequently performed and beloved operas in the world today.

events.title: Metropolitan Museum of Art Opens
events.summary: The Metropolitan Museum of Art officially opened its doors to the public in New York City.
events.details: Initially housed in a smaller building, it has grown into one of the world's largest and most significant art museums, preserving global culture and art for generations.

events.title: Tchaikovsky's "Swan Lake" Ballet Premieres
events.summary: Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky's iconic ballet, "Swan Lake," premiered at the Bolshoi Theatre in Moscow.
events.details: Though its initial reception was mixed, subsequent revivals cemented its legacy. It has since become one of the most popular and celebrated ballets in the classical repertoire.

events.title: First Woman Sets Foot on Antarctica
events.summary: Danish-Norwegian explorer Caroline Mikkelsen became the first woman to set foot on Antarctica.
events.details: While accompanying her husband on an expedition, she landed on an island of the continent. Her achievement was a pioneering and inspiring step for women in polar exploration.

events.title: Birth of Parícutin Volcano Witnessed
events.summary: A volcano, later named Parícutin, began to erupt from a cornfield in Mexico.
events.details: This remarkable event allowed scientists to document the full life cycle of a volcano for the very first time. It is considered one of the Natural Wonders of the World.

events.title: John Glenn Orbits the Earth
events.summary: Astronaut John Glenn became the first American to orbit the Earth aboard the Friendship 7 capsule.
events.details: His nearly five-hour flight was a monumental achievement for the U.S. space program. It boosted national morale and was a significant step forward for human space exploration.

events.title: Ranger 8 Transmits Images from Moon
events.summary: NASA's Ranger 8 spacecraft sent back over 7,000 high-quality images of the Moon.
events.details: Before its planned impact, the probe transmitted crucial, detailed photographs of the lunar surface. This data proved vital for selecting safe landing sites for the future Apollo missions.

events.title: Mir Space Station Core Module Launched
events.summary: The Soviet Union launched the core module of the Mir space station into orbit.
events.details: Mir became the first continuously inhabited long-term research station in space. It hosted international crews and enabled groundbreaking scientific experiments for fifteen years, paving the way for future collaboration.

events.title: Tara Lipinski Wins Olympic Gold Medal
events.summary: American figure skater Tara Lipinski won the Olympic gold medal at just 15 years old.
events.details: At the Nagano Winter Olympics, she became the youngest-ever Olympic champion in individual figure skating. Her joyful and technically brilliant performance captivated audiences worldwide and inspired many young athletes.

events.title: Spain Ratifies European Constitution in Referendum
events.summary: Spanish citizens voted in a referendum to ratify the proposed European Constitution.
events.details: Spain was the first country to hold a public vote on the treaty. The strong 'yes' vote demonstrated public support for greater European integration, cooperation, and democratic participation.

events.title: New Species of Tree Frog Discovered
events.summary: Scientists announced the discovery of a new 'dwarf' tree frog species in the Andes.
events.details: Named Hyloscirtus princecharlesi in honor of Prince Charles' conservation work, the discovery highlighted the incredible biodiversity of the region and the importance of ongoing environmental protection efforts.

events.title: First Bionic Eye Patient Regains Sight
events.summary: A patient who was completely blind received the first Argus II 'bionic eye' implant.
events.details: The revolutionary retinal prosthesis system allowed the patient to perceive patterns of light and shapes. This marked a major breakthrough in restoring partial sight to people with specific forms of blindness.

events.title: Ukraine Parliament Votes to End Violence
events.summary: Ukraine's parliament voted to condemn violence and withdraw troops from the capital, Kyiv.
events.details: During the climax of the Revolution of Dignity, this parliamentary resolution was a crucial step toward de-escalation. It represented a powerful move to end bloodshed and seek a peaceful resolution.

events.title: Major Study Links Green Spaces to Longevity
events.summary: A new study affirmed the link between urban green spaces and increased life expectancy.
events.details: Published on this day, the comprehensive study provided strong evidence that access to parks and nature in cities has a significant positive impact on public health and well-being.
